title: IMGUI State Model
use_for: defining retained state over IMGUI drawing
separating domain state from UI state
deciding keying and lifetime rules for windows and panels
designing binding and dirty-state models for partial UI updates
do_not_use_for: render performance work without state concerns
rules: store persistent UI state outside draw methods
separate domain truth from transient interaction state
key state by stable identity, not incidental call position
define reset and lifetime rules explicitly
track change boundaries so updates can target the smallest affected subtree
treat binding state and dirty markers as retained state with explicit ownership
procedure: List UI facts that must survive across IMGUI passes.
Split domain data from view-only state.
Assign stable identities for windows, panels, and repeated items.
Define ownership, lifetime, reset, and persistence boundaries.

primary_when: the request is specifically about retained state, view-model boundaries, or UI lifetime policy for IMGUI
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about overall framework architecture or visual styling

solves: how to keep view state deterministic and debuggable
how to prevent state leakage between tools, windows, and panels
how to model bindings and dirty propagation without losing explicit ownership

core_principles: Treat IMGUI drawing as a projection of retained state.
Keep domain state authoritative and UI state subordinate.
Model ephemeral interaction separately from durable user intent.
Keep binding state separate from both domain truth and rendered resources.
Use explicit dirty markers or version stamps to localize updates.
constraints: Repeated collections require stable keys across reorder and filtering.
Window and panel state ownership must be explicit at the host boundary.
Reset behavior must be defined for domain changes, hot reload, and tool close.
Dirty propagation rules must be deterministic and reviewable.
Binding comparisons must be cheaper than blindly rebuilding the affected presentation.
decision_rules: Use local retained state for purely visual interaction such as foldouts and scroll positions.
Promote state to host-level ownership when multiple panels or windows depend on it.
Avoid state storage keyed only by draw order if the composition tree can change.
Use subtree-level dirty markers when only one region depends on a changing state slice.
Use binding snapshots or version stamps when domain changes arrive more often than the UI must recompute derived presentation.

preferred_patterns: view state records per window or panel
host-owned registries keyed by stable identities
binding records that cache last-seen values or versions
dirty flags attached to host, panel, and component subtree boundaries
avoid_patterns: hidden static state for all UI
domain models polluted with IMGUI-only flags
state restoration inferred from GUI layout order
bindings that always push updates regardless of source changes
whole-tree invalidation for local state changes

recipes.name: Separate domain model and inspector interaction state
recipes.when: Use when an inspector edits data but also needs foldouts, selection, and scroll.
steps: Keep editable domain data in the tool model.
Store foldouts, expansion, and temporary selection in inspector view state.
Track version or dirty state for the inspected target.
Reset view state only when identity changes, not on every redraw.
Recompute only the inspector subsections whose source state actually changed.

pitfalls: Using static dictionaries without lifetime ownership rules.
Resetting retained state accidentally during recompilation or tool reopen.
Binding UI state to unstable list indices.
Treating binding caches as if they were domain truth.
Marking every panel dirty when only one bound subtree changed.
review_checklist: Is UI-only state separated from domain truth?
Are keys stable under reorder and filtering?
Are reset and persistence semantics explicit?
Can state ownership be traced to a concrete host?
Are dirty markers scoped to meaningful subtree boundaries?
Can bindings skip recomputation when source data is unchanged?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI State Patterns
references.interpretation: Use this first for retained-state ownership, keying, and lifetime rules in IMGUI frameworks.
references.why: Use for practical retained-state patterns, ownership boundaries, and keying rules.

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Incremental Update Model
references.interpretation: Use this first when retained binding state, dirty flags, or partial subtree updates are central to the design.
references.why: Use for binding boundaries, dirty propagation, and selective update strategy.

references.title: Unity Scripting API: GUIUtility
references.interpretation: Use this to confirm focus and control identity mechanics that affect state ownership boundaries.
references.why: Use for focus and control identity concepts that influence retained UI state boundaries.
